methods.title: Scene Question / Answer
methods.invocation: Load for every scene card and scene brief.
methods.output_contract: The scene must begin with a concrete question and end with a costly answer.

methods.title: Professional Ritual As Gameplay
source_refs: public pattern: service ritual as gameplay
public pattern: repeated work action as narrative control
methods.invocation: Load when the VN has a job, service role, investigation procedure, or repeated work action.
methods.output_contract: The scene must contain a concrete repeatable work action that changes dialogue or state.

methods.title: Subtext From Want / Need Mismatch
methods.invocation: Load for dialogue-heavy scenes.
methods.output_contract: Every major exchange must track surface topic, hidden want, and unsafe word.

methods.title: Choice Mental Model
methods.invocation: Load before writing choices or consequences.
methods.output_contract: The player must be able to infer what kind of consequence the choice is listening for.

methods.title: Branch And Bottleneck With State
methods.invocation: Load for limited-scope branching scenes.
methods.output_contract: Branches may reconverge only if state changes remain visible later.

methods.title: Four-Layer Narrative Integration
methods.invocation: Load when checking whether mechanics, story, character, and theme are actually connected.
methods.output_contract: Each key scene must state gameplay action, character action, plot event, and theme pressure.

methods.title: Voice State Memory
methods.invocation: Load whenever characters speak or a scene changes relationship state.
methods.output_contract: Drafting must read character cards and runtime state before producing dialogue.

methods.title: Line Energy And Punctuation
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or revising dialogue when voice, punctuation, and line energy matter.
methods.output_contract: Every dialogue block must show character-specific sentence shape, punctuation intent, and actable pressure.

methods.title: Character Liveliness Bias
methods.invocation: Load whenever creating, revising, or drafting characters, especially if roles feel too flat, hard, or only functional.
methods.output_contract: Every major character must receive liveliness +1: a charm hook, a lively memory point, character-owned punctuation play, and at least one actable ordinary quirk.

methods.title: Domestic Gal Social Texture
methods.invocation: Load before writing Chinese VN scenes that need local social pressure, occupational texture, and non-floating character voice.
methods.output_contract: Every important scene must name a social machine, a mundane tool, a name consequence, and a relation cost.

methods.title: Sentence Kernel And Display Voice
methods.invocation: Load before drafting dialogue, narration, thoughts, document text, or readable previews.
methods.output_contract: Each major speaker must have sentence-engine rules, display-layer rules, and allowed unclosed line endings.

methods.title: Galgame Textbox And Production Script
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or validating Chinese Galgame/AVG scripts that must feel playable rather than novelistic.
methods.output_contract: Every scene must treat each row as a restricted-view textbox plus production cue, with clean display syntax and no prose-only drift.

methods.title: Click Unit Textbox Rhythm
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or revising click-to-advance VN/ADV rows.
methods.output_contract: Every serious row must have an inferable click function; split and merge decisions must follow screen-state, pressure, inference, choice, and payload rather than one-speaker-one-row habit.

methods.title: Interiority Is Not Narration
methods.invocation: Load before writing or revising thought rows, first-person passages, or psychological texture.
methods.output_contract: Thought rows must carry private inference, unsaid motive, choice pressure, or contradiction; camera-visible action and decorative object prose must be narration/stage instead.

methods.title: Non-Preachy Dialogue Pressure
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or revising dialogue that risks sounding like advice, craft theory, theme explanation, or AI-polished talk.
methods.output_contract: Dialogue must create actable pressure through request, refusal, bargain, mistake, object operation, relation cost, or choice state instead of explaining the thesis.

methods.title: Open Source Format Fusion
methods.invocation: Load before drafting, validating, or exporting scenes that need portable branch structure and character memory packages.
methods.output_contract: CSV remains the source of truth and must export to WebGAL, Ren'Py, Ink, Yarn, Godot Dialogue Manager, and Character Card V2 without losing branch/state intent.

methods.title: Novel Architecture Fusion
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or validating scenes that must belong to a larger novel/game structure.
methods.output_contract: Each scene must expose chapter goal, synopsis, POV pressure, narrative strands, character arcs, and foreshadow refs.

methods.title: Dialogue Display And Interiority
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or rendering scenes that need Gal-style dialogue marks, casual speech, and inner monologue.
methods.output_contract: Readable previews must render dialogue with `【角色】『台词』`, thought rows with `（内心文本）`, and dialogue must allow character-specific casual particles and pressure punctuation.

methods.title: Theme Spine And Playable Thesis
methods.invocation: Load before planning a project, route, chapter, or scene that must express a central theme through play.
methods.output_contract: The project must define a thesis question, repeated player behavior, cost, concrete object, route arguments, and final payoff.

methods.title: Storylet Quality And Stateful Microinteraction
methods.invocation: Load before writing choices, conditional scenes, investigation nodes, optional interactions, or repeated location loops.
methods.output_contract: Every quality, storylet, and microinteraction must declare condition, immediate feedback, state change, delayed callback, and production cue.

methods.title: Vertical Slice Production Pipeline
methods.invocation: Load before expanding a project beyond a test scene, and before delivering any new sample script.
methods.output_contract: A project must prove a small slice with draft-session, CSV, readable preview, QA, exports, state coverage, and memory updates.

methods.title: Full Script Corpus Study
methods.invocation: Load whenever a script claims to reference actual complete VN/Ren'Py works, or before writing a serious sample that needs corpus-grounded pacing.
methods.output_contract: The workbench must identify legal complete scripts analyzed, report aggregate metrics, state the structural lesson reused, and avoid copying source text.

methods.title: Corpus Metric Targeting
methods.invocation: Load before serious drafting that uses complete-script references, especially after a new corpus analysis round.
methods.output_contract: The draft must declare target textbox, dialogue/narration, choice/state, staging, and QA metrics before prose, then compare after validation.

methods.title: External Project Ingestion Protocol
methods.invocation: Load whenever the user asks the workbench to keep learning from external projects, mature tools, engine files, or open-source writing systems.
methods.output_contract: Every learning round must produce a source-log entry, a local reusable improvement, and a verification result.

methods.title: Source Adaptation Protocol
source_refs: user instruction: do not use other people's original files directly
methods.invocation: Load whenever external projects, methods, or skills are studied before they enter the active workbench.
methods.output_contract: Outside material must be rewritten into workbench-owned methods or skills before it can affect generation.

methods.title: Generation Context Assembly Order
methods.invocation: Load before any draft-session or generated prose so standards, project memory, scene task, character control, retrieval, and QA are assembled in a stable order.
methods.output_contract: The workbench must prove which local context surfaces were loaded and keep reusable methods separate from project-only generated memory.

methods.title: User Prompt Response Protocol
methods.invocation: Load before interpreting user instructions that ask for learning, writing, rewriting, continuing, expanding, or character setup.
methods.output_contract: The assistant must classify the newest prompt and route it to method updates, project memory, generated content, exports, or user-visible character briefs.

methods.title: Character Setting Presentation And Memory Points
methods.invocation: Load whenever creating, revising, or presenting characters, especially when the user needs visible memory points.
methods.output_contract: New or materially changed characters must have both machine cards and user-visible briefs with stable memory points, voice signatures, body signatures, and example lines.

methods.title: Storage Boundary Protocol
methods.invocation: Load before adding methods, project memory, generated scripts, exports, or QA artifacts.
methods.output_contract: Every new file must be placed in reusable core, project memory, generated content, exports, or QA.

methods.title: Rewrite Pipeline
methods.invocation: Load before creating a revised draft from an earlier script.
methods.output_contract: The rewrite must produce a new generated draft file and only update project memory after validation.

methods.title: Character Presence Revision
source_refs: user critique history internalized into workbench
methods.invocation: Load before revising a script for AI flavor, flat voice, over-polish, or weak character presence.
methods.output_contract: The revision must list changed row ids, reason for change, and whether character card/runtime state updates are required.

methods.title: Changed-Line Voice Integrity Check
methods.invocation: Load after drafting or rewriting, but apply the checklist only to lines changed by the assistant.
methods.output_contract: Changed rows must be checked for AI-tell risk, handprint preservation, and any required fix.

methods.title: Portable Skill Handoff Protocol
methods.invocation: Load when adding reusable capabilities or preparing the workbench for another AI.
methods.output_contract: A handoff-ready update must state new skill ids, new method ids, canonical path, handoff copy path, and validation result.

methods.title: Runtime Dialogue Pipeline
methods.invocation: Load before drafting, validating, or exporting any scene with dialogue, commands, choices, labels, jumps, or variables.
methods.output_contract: Every CSV row must map to a runtime unit, every choice must have a target and state effect, and exports must remain generated artifacts.

methods.title: Retrievable Character Memory Blocks
methods.invocation: Load before writing character dialogue, updating relationship state, creating character cards, or handing a project to another AI.
methods.output_contract: Drafting must separate stable card, runtime state, and episodic memory blocks, then update only canon-changing memories after validation.

methods.title: Plain Text Project Granularity
methods.invocation: Load when organizing a long story, creating project folders, preparing handoff files, or expanding a test scene.
methods.output_contract: Every project must remain readable as small stable files with summaries, status, tags, dependencies, changes, and callbacks.

methods.title: Passage Tag Route Map
methods.invocation: Load before planning branching routes, optional scenes, storylets, investigation nodes, or Twine/Yarn/Ink-style exports.
methods.output_contract: Scenes and branches must expose passage ids, route tags, centered characters, required state, created state, callback hooks, and export mapping.

methods.title: Two-Pass Narrative Lint
methods.invocation: Load before trusting a serious draft, expanding a route, handing a project to another AI, or claiming character voice/state is fixed.
methods.output_contract: Run deep-audit so graph, refs, state effects, and character-control layers align before final delivery.

methods.title: AI Native Workbench Entrypoint
methods.invocation: Load when using or extending the local web/AI entrypoint for the VN workbench.
methods.output_contract: AI calls must assemble local context and save raw responses under generated content; canon still requires CSV and QA.

methods.title: Workflow Runtime Status
methods.invocation: Load when showing project state, automating multiple steps, or recommending the next workbench action.
methods.output_contract: Every workflow step must expose input artifact, output artifact, status, command, and next recommended action.

methods.title: Style Asset Prompt Compiler
methods.invocation: Load before turning method cards, anti-AI rules, style notes, and character controls into an AI generation prompt.
methods.output_contract: Compile rules into context, style, character, anti_ai, output, and self_check blocks instead of passing raw notes.

methods.title: VN Quality Debt Attribution
methods.invocation: Load after drafting, rewriting, validating, or diagnosing a VN scene that mechanically passes but feels weak.
methods.output_contract: Classify remaining issues as VN-specific quality debt and route each debt type to a concrete repair pass.

methods.title: Codex Chat Creation Protocol
methods.invocation: Load whenever the user asks through chat to create, rewrite, expand, or test a VN/Galgame work.
methods.output_contract: Chat output must become project memory, scene design, script artifacts, and QA results before final delivery.

methods.title: Style Corpus Intake Protocol
methods.invocation: Load whenever the user adds tutorials, VN scripts, style references, or large learning corpora to the workbench.
methods.output_contract: Raw pending files must be normalized into learned Markdown, logged, then distilled into methods, style profiles, or project memory.

methods.title: Style Reference Profile Compiler
methods.invocation: Load after a batch of tutorials or VN scripts has been normalized and needs to become a compact style asset.
methods.output_contract: Produce a compact profile with metrics, sentence engine, display rules, emotional delivery, examples, repair moves, and QA checklist.

methods.title: SillyTavern + AnythingLLM Fusion
methods.invocation: Load when using large learned corpora together with stable VN character memory and dialogue generation.
methods.output_contract: Use AnythingLLM-style corpus ingestion/retrieval first, then SillyTavern-style character card/lorebook/runtime memory injection for drafting.

methods.title: Chinese VN Generation Hard Gates
methods.invocation: Load before every Chinese Galgame/AVG draft or rewrite, especially when checking language, emotion, branch state, click splitting, and thought rows.
methods.output_contract: Drafts must pass click-unit, dialogue-block, private-thought, Chinese display rhythm, character voice, branch-debt, and playable-theme gates before delivery.

methods.title: Tutorial Absorption To Generation Contract
methods.invocation: Load after tutorial intake and before Chinese Galgame/AVG drafting to prove the learned lessons are visible in generated rows.
methods.output_contract: Draft-session or project audit must map tutorial lessons to concrete evidence: click rhythm, dialogue blocks, private thoughts, character punctuation, local-life pressure, choice debt, and banned-pattern scan.

methods.title: Deep VN Corpus Application
methods.invocation: Load after complete-script corpus analysis and before serious Chinese Galgame/AVG drafting, especially when calibrating style, punctuation, click splitting, thought rows, options, branches, and route debt.
methods.output_contract: A draft must declare corpus rhythm target, dialogue block plan, narration placement, private thought pockets, speaker punctuation fingerprints, visible option count, branch state count, rejoin point, persistent debt, and tutorial cross-check.

methods.title: VN Style Handprint Fusion
methods.invocation: Load before serious Chinese Galgame/AVG drafting after any learned corpus intake. Use it to shape sentence movement, emotional transitions, character handprints, punctuation energy, and click-level prose feel.
methods.output_contract: The draft must show original sentence moves, character-specific breath, playable emotional turns, private thought pockets, and lively but non-copied VN dialogue rather than only passing hard rules.

methods.title: Private Thought Pocket Texture
methods.invocation: Load before writing or revising Chinese VN thought rows, especially when psychological texture feels mechanical, camera-visible, or AI-like.
methods.output_contract: Thought rows must expose private inference, hidden cost, branch debt, or choice pressure, and use 1-2 row thought pockets unless a longer pocket has irreversible function.

methods.title: Dialogue Block Ownership
methods.invocation: Load before every dialogue-heavy Chinese Galgame/AVG draft or rewrite, especially when preventing one-line ping-pong dialogue.
methods.output_contract: The draft must declare dialogue ownership plan, same-speaker run owner, run length target, interruption point, narration/thought hinge, and anti-ping-pong move.

methods.title: Literary Depth And Liveliness Control
source_refs: dialogic voice / heteroglossia theory craft note
methods.invocation: Load before serious Chinese Galgame/AVG drafting or revision when characters feel flat, psychology feels mechanical, prose lacks spirit, or dialogue lacks cute/casual human timing.
methods.output_contract: The draft must declare literary pressure, reversal, recognition point, character self-story under threat, live hook + half step, thought-pocket chain, cute/casual timing, and branch debt before prose.

methods.title: Soft Opening: Problem First, Identity Later
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or revising VN openings, especially when a scene starts with direct identity exposition.
methods.output_contract: The opening must begin with a problem-bearing object, place, sound, contradiction, or rule failure, then reveal identity through action, address, and relationship pressure.

methods.title: Interior Breath Relaxation
methods.invocation: Load before drafting or revising Chinese VN/Gal inner monologue when psychology feels stiff, too formal, too judge-like, or over-repaired into rhetorical questions.
methods.output_contract: Thought rows must include character-owned breath where appropriate: filler, relaxation, self-correction, particle, half joke, practical mask, and next-click effect rather than only clean declarative verdicts or repeated questions.

methods.title: Complete Delivery Package Contract
methods.invocation: Load before any user-facing delivery, handoff, or claim that a VN/Gal project is complete.
methods.output_contract: Delivery must include readable draft, source CSV, public CSV/Excel, engine exports, character exports, QA evidence, runtime state, memory logs, state delta, and route map, or explicitly be labeled as a quick preview only.
